chunks.text: Tyler Hou named 2026 Hertz Fellowship Finalist - EECS at Berkeley Math and CS student Tyler Hou was named a finalist for the 2026 Hertz Fellowship, one of the most prestigious and competitive awards in the nation for doctoral students in applied sciences, mathematics, and engineering. Selected from a pool of over 1,450 applicants, Hou is one of only 54 finalists advancing to the final round of interviews. The fellowship, awarded by the Fannie and John Hertz Foundation, is designed to empower the nation’s most promising young minds to pursue research with transformational impact. The 2026 class of Hertz Fellows will be officially announced in May. Related Links 54 Finalists Named for the 2026 Hertz Fellowships View Open Faculty Positions
